org.arakhne.neteditor.io.pdf

Enum PdfEndCaps

  • java.lang.Object
    • java.lang.Enum<PdfEndCaps>
      • org.arakhne.neteditor.io.pdf.PdfEndCaps
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    Serializable, Comparable<PdfEndCaps>

    public enum PdfEndCaps
extends Enum<PdfEndCaps>
    End Caps for stroke in PDF files.
    Since:
    15.0
    Version:
    17.0 2013-10-23 21:03:04
    Author:
    Stéphane GALLAND
    Maven Group Id:
    org.arakhne.neteditor
    Maven Artifact Id:
    neteditor-io

    • Enum Constant Summary

      Enum Constants 
      Enum Constant and Description
      BUTT
      Ends unclosed subpaths and dash segments with no added decoration.
      ROUND
      Ends unclosed subpaths and dash segments with a round decoration that has a radius equal to half of the width of the pen.
      SQUARE
      Ends unclosed subpaths and dash segments with a square projection that extends beyond the end of the segment to a distance equal to half of the line width.

    • Method Summary

      Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      static PdfEndCaps fromGenericType(Stroke.EndCap endCap)
      Replies the end caps from the generic end cap.
      Stroke.EndCap getGenericType()
      Replies the value of the end caps for the generic API.
      int pdf()
      Replies the value of the end caps for the PDF file.
      static PdfEndCaps valueOf(String name)
      Returns the enum constant of this type with the specified name.
      static PdfEndCaps[] values()
      Returns an array containing the constants of this enum type, in the order they are declared.

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Enum

        clone, compareTo, equals, finalize, getDeclaringClass, hashCode, name, ordinal, toString, valueOf

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        getClass, notify, notifyAll, wait, wait, wait

    • Enum Constant Detail

      • BUTT

        public static final PdfEndCaps BUTT
        Ends unclosed subpaths and dash segments with no added decoration.

      • ROUND

        public static final PdfEndCaps ROUND
        Ends unclosed subpaths and dash segments with a round decoration that has a radius equal to half of the width of the pen.

      • SQUARE

        public static final PdfEndCaps SQUARE
        Ends unclosed subpaths and dash segments with a square projection that extends beyond the end of the segment to a distance equal to half of the line width.

    • Method Detail

      • values

        public static PdfEndCaps[] values()
        Returns an array containing the constants of this enum type, in the order they are declared. This method may be used to iterate over the constants as follows: 
        for (PdfEndCaps c : PdfEndCaps.values())
    System.out.println(c);
        Returns:
        an array containing the constants of this enum type, in the order they are declared

      • valueOf

        public static PdfEndCaps valueOf(String name)
        Returns the enum constant of this type with the specified name. The string must match exactly an identifier used to declare an enum constant in this type. (Extraneous whitespace characters are not permitted.)
        Parameters:
        name - the name of the enum constant to be returned.
        Returns:
        the enum constant with the specified name
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - if this enum type has no constant with the specified name
        NullPointerException - if the argument is null

      • getGenericType

        public Stroke.EndCap getGenericType()
        Replies the value of the end caps for the generic API.
        Returns:
        the value of the end caps for the generic API.

      • pdf

        public int pdf()
        Replies the value of the end caps for the PDF file.
        Returns:
        the value of the end caps for the PDF file.

      • fromGenericType

        public static PdfEndCaps fromGenericType(Stroke.EndCap endCap)
        Replies the end caps from the generic end cap.
        Parameters:
        endCap -
        Returns:
        the end caps or null if the specified parameter does not corresponds to an end caps.
